01_initial_request.input_data: 4,000 native small-molecule-binding proteins (for initial docking), 85 experimental structures of NTF2-like proteins (for extracting residue-residue distance and orientation distributions), and synthetic luciferin substrates (diphenylterazine/DTZ and 2-deoxycoelenterazine).
01_initial_request.user_intent: To overcome the limitations of native luciferases (such as misfolding, low substrate specificity, and cofactor requirements) by designing de novo luciferases from scratch that are small, highly stable, well-expressed in cells, and highly specific to synthetic luciferins for use as molecular probes in bioassays and multiplexed imaging.
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: A small molecular weight (~13.9 kDa), high thermostability (melting temperature > 95 °C), and a high catalytic efficiency (kcat/Km = 10^6 M^-1 s^-1) on diphenylterazine, comparable to native luciferases but with much higher substrate specificity.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: 【背景】当前已知/已完成：Native NTF2 structures have a range of pocket sizes and shapes but contain non-ideal features like long loops that compromise stability。【问题/缺口】目前缺少/尚未解决：Large numbers of ideal NTF2-like structures with suitable pockets for DTZ binding。【决策】为了create large numbers of ideal NTF2-like structures, 选择使用trRosetta对2,000 naturally occurring NTF2s执行Monte Carlo searches in sequence space and structure prediction, 预期获得an essentially unlimited number of hallucinated NTF2 scaffolds with shape-complementary binding pockets.

parameters.search_method: Monte Carlo searches in sequence space
parameters.loss_function: confidence of the neural network in the predicted structure supplemented with a topology-specific loss function over core residue pair geometries
parameters.loop_regions: allowed the number of residues to vary

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Generated 1,615 family-wide hallucinated NTF2 scaffolds that provided more shape-complementary binding pockets for DTZ than native small-molecule-binding proteins.

references.relevance: Provided the unconstrained protein hallucination approach for loop and variable regions.
Provided Rosetta sequence-design approaches for structure-guided sequence optimization.
Described the trRosetta structure prediction neural network used to identify successful de-novo-designed proteins.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: 【背景】当前已知/已完成：1,615 family-wide hallucinated NTF2 scaffolds have been generated。【问题/缺口】目前缺少/尚未解决：Placement of catalytic residues to stabilize the anionic state of DTZ and lower the single-electron transfer (SET) energy barrier。【决策】为了design a shape-complementary catalytic site, 选择使用RifGen and RifDock对anionic DTZ conformers and hallucinated NTF2 scaffolds执行rotamer interaction fields (RIFs) enumeration and docking, 预期获得scaffolds with optimized side chain-DTZ interactions including a key arginine residue.

parameters.RIFs_grid: three-dimensional grids consisting of millions of placements of amino acid side chains making hydrogen-bonding and nonpolar interactions
parameters.key_catalytic_residue: arginine guanidinium group placed adjacent to the N1 atom of the imidazopyrazinone group
parameters.selection_cutoff: top 50,000 docks with the most favourable side chain-DTZ interactions

02_agent_trajectory.observation: An average of eight side-chain rotamers, including an arginine residue to stabilize the anionic imidazopyrazinone core, were positioned in each pocket. The top 50,000 docks were selected for sequence optimization.

references.relevance: Quantum chemistry calculations suggesting the chemiluminescent reaction proceeds through an anionic species.
Provided the RifGen method to enumerate rotamer interaction fields.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: 【背景】当前已知/已完成：Top 50,000 docks with favorable side chain-DTZ interactions are identified。【问题/缺口】目前缺少/尚未解决：The remainder of the sequence needs optimization for high-affinity binding and foldability while maintaining the catalytic geometry and structural specificity。【决策】为了optimize the sequence for high-affinity binding to DTZ, 选择使用RosettaDesign对the top 50,000 docks执行two-step sequence optimization and filtering, 预期获得a set of highly stable and specific designs ready for experimental screening.

parameters.step_1_constraints: identities of all RIF and HBNet residues kept fixed, surrounding residues optimized
parameters.step_2_constraints: RIF residue identities (except arginine) allowed to vary
parameters.relaxation: scaffold backbone, side chains and DTZ substrate allowed to relax in Cartesian space
parameters.filtering_criteria: ligand-binding energy, protein-ligand hydrogen bonds, shape complementarity and contact molecular surface

02_agent_trajectory.observation: 7,648 designs were selected and ordered as pooled oligos for experimental screening.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: 【背景】当前已知/已完成：7,648 computational designs for DTZ luciferases have been generated as pooled oligos。【问题/缺口】目前缺少/尚未解决：Experimental validation to identify which computational designs actually possess luciferase activity in vitro and in vivo。【决策】为了identify active luciferases, 选择使用E. coli expression and colony-based screening对the 7,648 designs执行in vivo expression and luminescence imaging, 预期获得clones with confirmed luciferase activity.

tool.name: E. coli expression and colony-based screening

parameters.screening_method: colony-based screening and 96-well plate expression confirmation

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Three active designs were identified. The most active, LuxSit, is 117 residues (13.9 kDa), highly expressed, soluble, monomeric, and not fully unfolded at 95 °C, with an emission peak around 480 nm.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: 【背景】当前已知/已完成：LuxSit was successfully designed for DTZ, establishing a working pipeline for de novo luciferases。【问题/缺口】目前缺少/尚未解决：Luciferases specific to another substrate, h-CTZ, which has a different molecular shape from DTZ。【决策】为了create h-CTZ-specific luciferases, 选择使用AlphaFold2 and ProteinMPNN对additional NTF2 superfamily scaffolds执行pocket matching, catalytic site installation, and sequence design, 预期获得active luciferases specific for the h-CTZ substrate.

parameters.scaffolds: additional set of NTF2 superfamily scaffolds with matching pocket shapes
parameters.catalytic_geometry: histidine and arginine substrate interaction geometries successful in the first round
parameters.filtering_criteria: AlphaFold2-predicted pLDDT, Cα RMSD, contact molecular surface and Rosetta-computed binding energies

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Identified 2 designs (HTZ3-D2 and HTZ3-G4) out of 46 that had luciferase activity with h-CTZ. Both were highly soluble, monodisperse, and monomeric.

references.relevance: Used AlphaFold2 for structure prediction and confidence scoring (pLDDT).
Used ProteinMPNN for sequence design to achieve better stability, solubility, and accuracy.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: 【背景】当前已知/已完成：LuxSit has confirmed activity but its catalytic efficiency could potentially be improved。【问题/缺口】目前缺少/尚未解决：Understanding of the specific contributions of active-site residues to catalysis and further optimization of the enzyme's turnover rate。【决策】为了optimize luciferase activity and understand catalysis, 选择使用Site-saturation mutagenesis (SSM)对LuxSit substrate-binding pocket residues执行systematic mutation and activity screening, 预期获得variants with higher photon flux and catalytic efficiency.

parameters.target_residues: each residue in the substrate-binding pocket
parameters.mutation_strategy: mutated to every other amino acid one at a time

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Identified A96M and M110V mutants which increased activity by 16-fold and 19-fold. Guided by this, LuxSit-i (R60S/A96L/M110V) was created, achieving a >100-fold higher photon flux and a catalytic efficiency (kcat/Km) of 10^6 M^-1 s^-1.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: 【背景】当前已知/已完成：LuxSit-i is a highly efficient and specific de novo luciferase in vitro。【问题/缺口】目前缺少/尚未解决：Validation of its utility as a genetic tag and reporter in live mammalian cells, and demonstration of its multiplexing capability due to high substrate specificity。【决策】为了evaluate expression, function, and multiplexing capability, 选择使用Mammalian cell culture and luminescence imaging对HEK293T cells expressing LuxSit-i and RLuc执行transient expression and substrate/spectral-resolved assays, 预期获得proof of high specificity and simultaneous pathway monitoring in live cells.

tool.name: Mammalian cell culture and luminescence imaging

parameters.activators: human tumour necrosis factor (TNF) and forskolin (FSK)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: LuxSit-i showed exquisite specificity (50-fold selectivity for DTZ over bis-CTZ, >100-fold over other luciferins). Successfully demonstrated simultaneous multiplexed reporting of NF-κB and cAMP-PKA signaling pathways in the same sample.

references.relevance: Highlighted the difficulty of controlling luciferase specificity with directed evolution, contrasting with the success of the de novo approach.

03_success_verification.validation_technique: In vitro biochemical characterization (SDS-PAGE, SEC, CD spectroscopy, luminescence kinetics) and in vivo mammalian cell assays (HEK293T live-cell imaging, multiplexed signaling pathway reporting).

Molecular Weight.interpretation: LuxSit is 117 residues, smaller than any previously described luciferase, meeting the goal of a small molecular weight.

Thermostability.interpretation: CD melting experiments showed the protein is not fully unfolded at 95 °C and regains full structure upon cooling, meeting the high thermostability goal.

Catalytic Efficiency (kcat/Km).interpretation: LuxSit-i achieved a catalytic efficiency of 10^6 M^-1 s^-1 on DTZ, comparable to native luciferases, meeting the quantifiable goal.

Substrate Specificity.interpretation: LuxSit-i exhibited 50-fold selectivity for DTZ over bis-CTZ and >100-fold over other luciferin substrates, far exceeding the specificity of native luciferases.

03_success_verification.final_verdict: The study successfully achieved the de novo design of artificial luciferases (LuxSit and its optimized variant LuxSit-i) from scratch. The designed enzymes perfectly met all quantifiable goals: they are extremely small (~13.9 kDa), highly thermostable (Tm > 95 °C), possess high catalytic efficiency (10^6 M^-1 s^-1) on the synthetic substrate DTZ, and exhibit exquisite substrate specificity that enables multiplexed bioassays in mammalian cells.
